The question is asking to determine which version of Windows Server 2012 has the same file service limits as the "foundation" version. To find out, we need to compare the values in the columns "essentials", "standard", and "datacenter" with the value in the "foundation" column for the row labeled "file services limits". We can do this by scanning the row and checking for matching values. If any column has the same listed limit ("1 standalone dfs root") as the "foundation" column, that version has the same file service limits.
The "Essentials" version of Windows Server 2012 has the same file service limits as the "Foundation" version, with both allowing "1 standalone dfs root". Therefore, the answer is Essentials.
